meta.title: UK Legal Pathways — SafePassage
meta.description: All legal routes to the United Kingdom — asylum, skilled work, study, family, and resettlement.

hero.title: Legal Pathways to the United Kingdom
hero.sub: The UK offers multiple legal routes to live, work, study and seek protection. Every pathway on this page is authorised, safe, and free of dangerous crossings. Choose the route that matches your situation.

cards.name: Asylum
cards.desc: If you are fleeing persecution or war, you have a legal right to claim asylum in the UK. The process is free and protected by international law.

cards.name: Skilled Worker Visa
cards.desc: The UK actively recruits skilled professionals in healthcare, tech, engineering, and more. A job offer from a licensed UK employer is the key requirement.

cards.name: Student Visa
cards.desc: Accepted to a UK university or college? You can enter legally on a student visa with the right to work part-time and stay 2–3 years after graduation.

cards.name: Family Visa
cards.desc: If a close family member is a British citizen or has leave to remain in the UK, you may be able to join them legally through family reunification.

cards.name: Resettlement Schemes
cards.desc: The UK runs official resettlement programmes for the most vulnerable refugees — UKRS, ACRS, ARAP, and Homes for Ukraine. These bring people to the UK safely, on a plane, for free.

cards.name: Innovator Founder Visa
cards.desc: Start your own innovative, scalable business in the UK. No employer needed — you are the founder. Settle permanently after 3 years with an endorsement from an approved body.

cards.name: Global Talent Visa
cards.desc: For leaders and potential leaders in digital technology, research, or arts and culture. No job offer needed. Settle after 3 years (Exceptional Talent) or 5 years (Exceptional Promise).
